dailyJudgementSnippet: Anthropic drew the clearest direct approval on the front page: commenters called its alignment post valuable and actionable, while Claude also gained visibility from Xcode support and from Sonnet/Claude Code being treated as reference points in a major coding-model discussion [E1][E2][E3].
winnerExplanation: Anthropic won because it had the strongest combination of explicit praise and repeated relevant mentions. Its alignment write-up received clear positive commentary, Claude picked up ecosystem credibility from Xcode support, and Anthropic models were used as the benchmark in a prominent coding thread rather than just being mentioned in passing [E1][E2][E3].

openai.judgementSnippet: Codex showed up mainly as a benchmark for coding agents: one large thread treated Codex CLI as a useful comparison point for local tools, and the Xcode discussion referenced Codex as one of the agents people might otherwise be locked into, but the praise was limited and mostly indirect [E4][E5].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Commenters described the misalignment post as valuable, actionable, and informative, and the same day Claude gained added relevance from Xcode support while Sonnet and Claude Code were used as the standard of comparison in a prominent coding-model thread [E1][E2][E3].

evidence.summary: Anthropic's own alignment story drew directly favorable comments, including that it was 'valuable,' 'actionable,' and 'informative,' with additional discussion of Haiku, Sonnet, and Opus as practical model choices.

In the Xcode agent thread, commenters explicitly noted that 'Apple’s Xcode now supports the Claude Agent SDK,' giving Claude visible first-party IDE integration even though the thread's main excitement was broader agent interoperability.

The Qwen coding-model thread repeatedly used Anthropic products as the reference standard, with commenters comparing performance to Sonnet 4.5 and describing Claude Code as the kind of coding agent local models still struggle to match.
OpenAI appeared through Codex in the Qwen thread, where commenters cited Codex CLI as a useful benchmark for coding-agent quality and shared a guide for running workflows with Codex locally.
The Xcode discussion mentioned Codex as one of the agent options users might otherwise be locked into, keeping OpenAI in the conversation but without strong praise or criticism.
